methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $29M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $8.3B - $25.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Reflection AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $25B

private.name: Applied Intuition
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $15B

private.name: Together AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $8.3B

data.narrative:
Atmo is a Series A stage AI & Software company founded by Alex Levy and Johan Mathe that has raised $29.1M to date. The peer universe consists of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with direct stage and sector matches including Reflection AI, Applied Intuition, and Together AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $29.1M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $58M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against three same-stage, same-sector companies: Reflection AI at $25.0B, Applied Intuition at $15.0B, and Together AI at $8.3B. The median of this set is $15.0B, with a range of $8.3B to $25.0B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods produces a low of $49M, a median of $7.5B, and a high of $17.2B. The extreme divergence between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects a material gap: the peer set commands substantially higher valuations than post-Series A step-ups on capital raised alone would suggest. Key uncertainties include whether Atmo's metrics and market position justify valuation parity with the peer set, and whether the peer universe adequately represents frontier AI market conditions versus sustainable fundamentals.
